metadata.title: EMA Guideline on the development of new medicinal products for the treatment of Crohn's Disease (Revision 2)
metadata.note: Guideline adopted by CHMP 28 June 2018, in effect 1 January 2019. Supersedes Rev. 1. Covers induction and maintenance of remission in adult and paediatric CD, including fistulising CD.

rules.rule_text: Active Crohn's disease must be confirmed by evidence of active mucosal inflammation via recent endoscopy and/or imaging performed within 3 months before study entry; histological evaluation is expected.
rules.verbatim_quote: Active CD requires evidence of active mucosal inflammation by a recent (within 3 months) endoscopy and/or imaging; histological evaluation is expected.

rules.rule_text: Patient eligibility for CD trials must be defined within one or more of the following categories: (1) primary non-responders (refractory) to conventional treatment; (2) patients intolerant to conventional treatment; (3) steroid-dependent patients; (4) secondary non-responders to biologics. The specific population must be pre-specified in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patient categories include: refractory CD (primary non-response), intolerant to prior treatment, steroid-dependent, and secondary non-response (biologics).

rules.rule_text: For studies in CD remission, mucosal healing must be defined as the absence of macroscopic signs of active inflammation confirmed by endoscopy, combined with no or very mild symptoms.
rules.verbatim_quote: CD in remission: mucosal healing (MH) = absence of macroscopic signs of active inflammation by endoscopy + no/very mild symptoms.

rules.rule_text: The CDAI (Crohn's Disease Activity Index) must NOT be used as a primary endpoint due to concerns about its reliability and validity. Symptom assessment and endoscopic assessment of mucosal inflammation must be evaluated independently as co-primary endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: CDAI use as primary endpoint is discouraged due to reliability/validity concerns. Symptoms and inflammation must be evaluated independently as co-primary endpoints (PRO for symptoms + endoscopy for mucosal healing).

rules.rule_text: Co-primary endpoints must be: (1) the proportion of patients achieving symptomatic remission, assessed using a validated patient-reported outcome (PRO) instrument; and (2) the proportion of patients achieving endoscopic remission. Both must be met for the trial to be considered successful.
rules.verbatim_quote: Co-primary endpoints: proportion with symptomatic remission + proportion with endoscopic remission.
exceptions: Pending full validation of PRO instrument, PRO2 or PRO3 derived from CDAI diary items may be used as interim measures

rules.rule_text: Patient-reported outcome instruments for symptom assessment must be validated. In the interim, PRO2 (stool frequency + abdominal pain) or PRO3 (stool frequency + abdominal pain + general well-being) derived from CDAI diary items may be used, pending full instrument validation.
rules.verbatim_quote: PRO instruments: validated instrument required; in interim, PRO2 or PRO3 from CDAI diary items may be used.

rules.rule_text: Endoscopic assessment of mucosal inflammation must use a validated scale — either the Crohn's Disease Endoscopic Index of Severity (CDEIS) or the Simple Endoscopic Score for Crohn's Disease (SES-CD). Magnetic resonance enterography (MRE) cannot be used as a primary outcome measure for mucosal assessment until formally validated for this purpose.
rules.verbatim_quote: Mucosal inflammation: CDEIS or SES-CD validated scales required; MRE cannot be used as outcome measure until validated.

rules.rule_text: Estimands must be pre-specified in the trial protocol in accordance with ICH E9(R1). In long-term studies, treatment discontinuation must be handled using a composite strategy (counted as treatment failure). Use of rescue medication must also be pre-specified as treatment failure in the estimand.
rules.verbatim_quote: Estimands (ICH E9(R1)): must be pre-specified; treatment discontinuation in long-term studies = treatment failure (composite strategy); rescue medication use = treatment failure.

rules.rule_text: Secondary endpoints must include: (1) combined mucosal healing plus symptomatic remission; (2) symptomatic response; (3) steroid-free remission; (4) histological evaluation; (5) faecal calprotectin; and (6) time to remission.
rules.verbatim_quote: Secondary endpoints: both MH + symptomatic remission combined; response; remission without steroids; histological evaluation; faecal calprotectin; time to remission.

rules.rule_text: Dose-finding studies must be randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, and parallel-group, with a minimum duration of 4–8 weeks. Multiple doses must be evaluated to characterise the dose-response relationship.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dose-finding: ≥4-8 weeks, placebo-controlled, randomized, double-blind, parallel group; multiple doses evaluated.

rules.rule_text: At least two confirmatory trials are required to support a general 'treatment of Crohn's disease' indication claim. Confirmatory trials must be randomised and double-blind, and must include either an active comparator or placebo.
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmatory: at least two confirmatory trials required for general 'treatment of CD' claim; randomized, double-blind; active comparator and/or placebo.

rules.rule_text: Short-term induction efficacy must typically be assessed over 6–12 weeks. The overall study duration must be at least 12 months (inclusive of the short-term induction phase). Treat-through or re-randomisation designs are acceptable for the maintenance phase.
rules.verbatim_quote: Short-term efficacy (induction): 6-12 weeks typical; long-term: total duration ≥12 months (including short-term phase); treat-through or re-randomisation designs acceptable.

rules.rule_text: For a maintenance claim, the sponsor must demonstrate that patients in complete remission at the end of the induction phase remain in remission throughout a 52-week maintenance study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Maintenance claim: must demonstrate patients in complete remission remain in remission across 52-week study.

rules.rule_text: Placebo-controlled study duration must not exceed 6 months; escape procedures must be implemented for patients who fail to respond or worsen, and must be pre-defined in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Placebo control max 6 months recommended (with escape procedures).

rules.rule_text: For a first-line treatment claim, the confirmatory trial must include a direct comparison against an accepted standard first-line treatment. For a second-line claim, a placebo-controlled design is acceptable.
rules.verbatim_quote: Comparator: first-line claim requires direct comparison with accepted standard first-line treatment; second-line = placebo acceptable.

rules.rule_text: Concomitant immunosuppressant therapy (azathioprine/mercaptopurine) must be stable for at least 3 months before study enrolment. Corticosteroids must be tapered according to a fixed, pre-specified schedule within the first 12 weeks of the maintenance phase.
rules.verbatim_quote: Concomitant treatment: AZA/MP stable ≥3 months before inclusion; steroids must be tapered per fixed schedule within first 12 weeks of maintenance.

rules.rule_text: Pharmacokinetic studies must cover special populations including elderly patients, paediatric patients, and patients with renal or hepatic impairment. Drug interaction studies are mandatory for anticipated co-medications, particularly glucocorticoids and immunosuppressants.
rules.verbatim_quote: PK must cover special populations (elderly, paediatric, renal/hepatic); interaction studies mandatory for co-medications (glucocorticoids, immunosuppressants).

rules.rule_text: For immunomodulatory or immunosuppressive biologics, special attention must be given to: serious and opportunistic infections, autoimmune manifestations, and tumour facilitation potential. Long-term immunogenicity assessment (both binding and neutralising antibodies) and re-treatment outcomes must be evaluated.
rules.verbatim_quote: Immunomodulators: special attention to serious infections, autoimmune diseases, tumour facilitation potential; long-term: immunogenicity (binding/neutralizing antibodies), re-treatment outcomes.

rules.rule_text: A Risk Management Plan (RMP) is mandatory for all immunosuppressive or immunomodulating biologics used in CD. The RMP must include monitoring provisions for infections and malignancies as identified risks.
rules.verbatim_quote: Risk management plan: mandatory for immunosuppressive/immunomodulating biologics; monitor infections and malignancies.

rules.rule_text: Paediatric CD studies must include patients from 2 years of age unless safety signals preclude it. Population pharmacokinetic modelling is preferred over separate PK studies in children. Endoscopic evaluation is mandatory — symptomatic response alone is insufficient for the paediatric population.
rules.verbatim_quote: Paediatrics: include from 2 years unless safety signals preclude it; population PK modelling preferred; endoscopy required (not symptomatic response alone).

rules.rule_text: In paediatric CD trials, growth velocity must be included as a secondary endpoint, and a minimum 2-year observation period for growth effects is required. Magnetic resonance enterography (MRE) is preferred over CT enterography (CTE) to minimise radiation exposure.
rules.verbatim_quote: Growth velocity secondary endpoint; MRE preferred over CTE; 2-year observation for growth effects.

rules.rule_text: Studies in fistulising Crohn's disease must use fistula closure and maintenance of fistula closure as the primary endpoint. MRI evaluation is required to confirm fistula status. Studies must be at least 12 months in duration.
rules.verbatim_quote: Fistulising CD: primary endpoint = fistula closure + maintenance of closure; MRI required; studies ≥12 months.

rules.rule_text: Studies in elderly CD patients must enrol adequate numbers of elderly subjects. The study protocol must specifically address: GFR-related dose adjustments, increased susceptibility to adverse events in older patients, and polypharmacy drug-drug interaction potential.
rules.verbatim_quote: Elderly: adequate numbers required; GFR reduction, adverse event susceptibility, polypharmacy DDI must be addressed.

rules.rule_text: The treatment goal for CD trials must be achieving and maintaining both symptomatic AND endoscopic remission. Symptomatic response or relief alone is not an acceptable primary treatment goal for regulatory purposes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Treatment goal: achieving and maintaining symptomatic AND endoscopic remission (goal, not just symptom relief).

rules.rule_text: Statistical analysis plans must be aligned with the pre-specified estimands in accordance with ICH E9(R1) and must be finalised before database lock. All intercurrent events and their handling strategies must be pre-specified in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Statistics: aligned with estimands; must be pre-specified in protocol.
